title: SUNCO CROSS-DOMAIN CROSS-DOMAIN-BLOCK
description: Schema for the YAML block fenced inside the <!-- SUNCO:CROSS-DOMAIN-BLOCK-START --> … <!-- SUNCO:CROSS-DOMAIN-BLOCK-END --> region of .planning/domains/contracts/CROSS-DOMAIN.md produced by cross-domain-sync (Phase 48/M4.1). Deterministic, no-LLM generation: UI-SPEC.md + API-SPEC.md SPEC-BLOCK extraction, set-intersection + type-shape comparison, template serialization. Per spec §8 Phase 4.1. BS1 discipline: version const: 1, additionalProperties: true so Phase 49/M4.2 verify-gate cross-domain layer (missing-endpoint, type-drift, error-state-mismatch, orphan-endpoint findings) and later phases may extend without a schema bump. generated_from.sha is the source file content SHA-256 (NOT a git commit SHA) so regeneration is idempotent across commits without a working copy change. minItems: 0 per projected array — Phase 48 is a generator, Phase 49 verify-gate judges emptiness/coverage.

version.description: Schema version per BS1. Constant 1 for Phase 48 output. Future schema bumps increment this and ship coordinated migrations, same discipline as api/data/event/ops/ui schemas.

generated_from.description: Provenance of the generated block. One entry per source SPEC file actually consumed by this generation (minimum: UI-SPEC.md + API-SPEC.md when both are required). Ordered by read order.

spec.description: Repository-relative path to the source SPEC.md file (e.g., '.planning/domains/frontend/UI-SPEC.md'). Full path, not basename.

sha.description: SHA-256 of the source file's raw bytes at read time. Lowercase hex, 64 chars. Not a git commit SHA — content hash so regeneration is idempotent across commits without file change.

endpoints_consumed.description: Endpoints the UI surface declares it consumes. Projected from UI-SPEC.md SPEC-BLOCK endpoints_consumed. minItems: 0 — a UI may consume zero endpoints (pure static content); coverage judgment is Phase 49 verify-gate scope.

ui_ref.description: UI component or view name that consumes this endpoint (e.g., 'UserDashboard').

endpoints_defined.description: Endpoints the API surface defines. Projected from API-SPEC.md SPEC-BLOCK endpoints. minItems: 0 — emptiness is Phase 49 scope (orphan-endpoint finding applies in reverse direction).

owner_spec.description: Full repository-relative path of the SPEC.md that defines this endpoint (e.g., '.planning/domains/backend/API-SPEC.md'). Full path, not basename.

error_mappings.description: Cross-projection of API error envelope codes against UI error_states_handled. Each entry pairs an API-declared error code with the UI state that handles it (or 'fallback' when no explicit handler is declared and the UI falls back to a generic state). minItems: 0 — Phase 48 projects what the two specs declare; Phase 49 error-state-mismatch finding is the coverage judge.

api_code.description: API error code from API-SPEC.md SPEC-BLOCK error_envelope or endpoints[].errors[].code (e.g., 'AUTH_EXPIRED').

ui_state.description: UI state name from UI-SPEC.md SPEC-BLOCK error_states_handled that handles this code (e.g., 'relogin-prompt'). Empty string when no explicit handler and only fallback applies.

fallback.description: UI fallback state name when no explicit mapping exists (e.g., 'generic-error'). Projected from UI-SPEC.md error_states_handled convention; empty string when UI spec has no declared fallback.

type_contracts.description: Type-shape matches between UI-consumed field paths and API-defined field paths. Projected from UI-SPEC.md type references (where present) against API-SPEC.md endpoints[].response_schema. Phase 48 records structural declaration pairs; Phase 49 type-drift finding is the correctness judge.

ui_type.description: Type as declared by UI-SPEC.md. Empty string when UI spec does not declare an explicit type at this path.

api_type.description: Type as declared by API-SPEC.md. Empty string when API spec does not declare an explicit type at this path.

match.description: true when ui_type and api_type are equal (case-sensitive) and both non-empty. false when either is empty or when strings differ. Phase 48 is a structural matcher, not a type-system-aware matcher — Phase 49 heuristic review handles nuance (nullability, unions, aliases).
